zh.speaker: A
zh.text: 上周我去看了网上吹得特别火的那部科幻新片，你猜观感咋样？

zh.speaker: B
zh.text: 应该很不错吧？我刷到好多博主都在夸。

zh.speaker: A
zh.text: 也就普普通通吧，特效还过得去，剧情完全经不起推敲。

zh.speaker: B
zh.text: 啊？我本来还打算周末买票去看呢。对了，之前刷到的那家新开的网红泰餐呢？我还想去打卡。

zh.speaker: A
zh.text: 也是一般般，冬阴功汤淡得像兑水，咖喱鸡也没入味。

zh.speaker: B
zh.text: 合着你这周出门消费踩的全是雷啊，可太惨了。

en.speaker: A
en.text: I watched that super hyped new sci-fi movie last week, guess how it was?

en.speaker: B
en.text: It should be great, right? I've seen tons of influencers praising it.

en.speaker: A
en.text: It's just **mid** to be honest, the effects are okay but the plot doesn't hold up at all.

en.speaker: B
en.text: Huh? I was planning to buy tickets for it this weekend. Oh by the way, what about that newly opened viral Thai restaurant? I wanted to check it out.

en.speaker: A
en.text: That's also **mid**, the tom yum soup is so watery and the curry chicken has no flavor.

en.speaker: B
en.text: Sounds like all the money you spent going out this week was a total waste, that sucks.

definitions.en: (Internet slang) Used to describe something that is mediocre, unimpressive or of lower quality than expected
definitions.zh: （网络俚语）形容事物平庸普通、没有亮点，质量低于预期

definitions.en: Literally refers to the middle or central part of something
definitions.zh: 本义指某个事物的中间、中部

examples.en: That new drama everyone is talking about is so mid, I gave up after 3 episodes.
examples.zh: 大家都在讨论的那部新剧太一般了，我看了三集就弃了。

examples.en: I thought the limited edition snack would taste good, but it's actually pretty mid.
examples.zh: 我本以为那款限定零食会很好吃，结果其实挺普通的。

examples.en: Don't bother going to that mid café, their coffee tastes like burnt water.
examples.zh: 别费劲去那家普普通通的咖啡馆了，他们的咖啡喝起来像烧糊的水。
